notes: 21 VCGs across 8 sub-groups (5+4+3+5+3+3+1+1). All 691 is_relevant verses assigned. 9 missing meanings handled: vc_id 64987 (Psa 116:8 dim.ah) assigned to M03-A-VCG-02; 8 BOUNDARY missing meanings (che.vel-M) assigned to M03-BOUNDARY-VCG-01. Dual-membership notes recorded in verse_id lists where applicable — primary VCG governs verse routing per Phase 6. Secondary VCG designations are term-level only per brief §8.

vcgs.label: Bereavement and Personal Loss Weeping
vcgs.description: Weeping arising from the death of, separation from, or irrecoverable loss of a loved one — the immediate, deeply personal grief response of bereavement. Weeping flows from a broken bond or absent beloved; it is relational and often shared communally as recognition of the loss of one who was valued.

vcgs.label: Penitential and Repentant Weeping
vcgs.description: Weeping directed toward God as an expression of contrition, repentance, confession, or urgent intercession — weeping that is Godward in orientation and that either moves God to respond or signals a genuine inner turning toward him.

vcgs.label: Communal and National Grief Weeping
vcgs.description: Weeping that is communal, national, or civic — the collective inner grief response to shared catastrophe, threatened destruction, divine judgment on the community, or national loss.

vcgs.label: The Prophet's Compassionate Weeping for His People
vcgs.description: Weeping arising from prophetic identification — the prophet or witness who weeps on behalf of a people or nation, entering into their grief from the outside as a compassionate witness-griever.

vcgs.label: Eschatological Weeping — Judgment Anguish and End-of-Tears Promise
vcgs.description: Weeping that is eschatological in character — either the defining inner anguish of those under divine judgment, or the eschatological promise that grief will cease when God wipes every tear away.

vcgs.label: Mourning for the Dead
vcgs.description: Personal and communal mourning rites that accompany death — the structured, socially-recognised expression of grief at the loss of a person, with recognizable forms: mourning periods, garments, lament cries, and prescribed rituals.

vcgs.label: Mourning over National Disaster and Divine Judgment
vcgs.description: Mourning arising from communal catastrophe — enemy invasion, famine, exile, national defeat, or the direct intervention of divine judgment upon a people. Collective, often unavoidable inner-and-outward mourning that fills every public space.

vcgs.label: Divinely Governed Mourning — Commanded, Withheld, and Eschatologically Reversed
vcgs.description: Mourning in which God is the active agent governing whether it occurs, is suppressed, or is transformed — mourning commanded as fitting response to judgment, withheld as itself a judgment, or transformed into joy.

vcgs.label: New Testament Mourning — Commanded, Eschatological, and Pastoral
vcgs.description: The mourning vocabulary distinctive to the New Testament: commanded grief over sin, mourning at Christ's departure, pastoral mourning over unrepentant communities, eschatological mourning of those who see the pierced one, and mourning's abolition in the new creation.

vcgs.label: Soul and Heart-Located Sorrow — The Inner Condition
vcgs.description: Grief as a settled, located inner condition — sorrow felt in the soul, heart, and deepest inner being; the weight carried inwardly as an ongoing state before or beneath any outward expression.

vcgs.label: Godly Grief — Morally Generative Sorrow and Grief over Sin
vcgs.description: Grief that is morally motivated and productive — sorrow arising from witnessing or experiencing moral failure, sin, or relational rupture, leading toward moral action, repentance, or change. Includes the Spirit's grief as the measure of sin's relational damage.

vcgs.label: Christ's Gethsemane Grief — Soul-Sorrow to the Depth of Death
vcgs.description: The unique inner-being grief of Jesus in Gethsemane — soul-located sorrow that reaches the extreme depth of threatening life itself, explicitly named as perilupos ('very sorrowful, even to death').

vcgs.label: Distress Driving Prayer — The Psalmic Pattern
vcgs.description: The dominant pattern of M03-D's corpus: distress as the inner-being crisis that drives the sufferer to cry out to God, and God's answering deliverance. Distress is the state that activates prayer; the pattern is distress → prayer → divine answer.

vcgs.label: Distress in the Soul and Spirit — Inner-Location Named
vcgs.description: Verses that explicitly locate distress in the soul, spirit, or heart of the sufferer — the most directly inner-being evidenced instances, where the inner location of anguish is named by the text.

vcgs.label: Distress as Covenantal Discipline — The Turning Will
vcgs.description: Distress in its disciplinary function — distress that God uses as an instrument to press the inner will back toward himself, producing repentance, renewed covenantal seeking, or divine solidarity with the suffering people.

vcgs.label: Prophetic Distress Oracles — The Day of Trouble
vcgs.description: Distress as a feature of prophetic announcements and oracles — the 'day of distress,' the time of trouble announced for nations and communities under divine judgment, as an anticipated, proclaimed, and all-encompassing condition.

vcgs.label: Greek Anguish — Relational, Afterlife, and Eschatological
vcgs.description: The Greek anguish vocabulary in M03-D — terms carrying the anguish register in distinctly New Testament and eschatological contexts: personal relational loss, afterlife suffering, apostolic pastoral pain, and eschatological communal dread.

vcgs.label: Oppression Groaning — Suffering's Cry Rising to God
vcgs.description: Groaning and sighing under suffering, oppression, or devastating circumstance — the involuntary vocal expression of inner grief that rises to God and moves him to act covenantally or with compassion.

vcgs.label: Spirit and Creation Groaning — Rom 8 and Apostolic Travail
vcgs.description: The theologically densest groaning texts: the Spirit's intercession in groanings too deep for words within the believer's spirit, creation's collective groan oriented toward redemption, and the apostolic travail of purposeful love-anguish.

vcgs.label: Personal Sighing — Grief's Audible Weight in the Individual
vcgs.description: The personal, individual sighing of Job and the psalmists — grief made audible as a continuous, exhausting, bodily-pervading inner burden that crowds out everything else and is laid openly before God.

vcgs.label: The Servant's Pain and Pain Before God — Identified Suffering
vcgs.description: Pain that is defined, perceived, or held by God — pain that constitutes an identity or is acknowledged as real by the divine; including the suffering servant's identity-defining sorrow and the verses where God sees and responds to pain.

vcgs.label: Unceasing Inner Pain — The Wound Without Healing
vcgs.description: Pain characterised by persistence, resistance to remedy, and its erosive effect on the inner person and the divine relationship — pain that does not heal and corrodes trust, coexisting beneath outward expressions of joy.

vcgs.label: Birth-Pangs as Metaphor for Acute Inner Anguish
vcgs.description: Pain vocabulary using the birth-pangs image (che.vel-B) to describe the acute, involuntary, sudden seizure of inner anguish that cannot be controlled or resisted — overwhelming, physical, and inescapable.

vcgs.label: Bitterness of Soul — Grief's Sharpest Inner Quality
vcgs.description: Bitterness as the specific inner quality that grief acquires at its most intense — bitterness-of-soul arising from suffering, loss, divine dealings, and relational wounding; what grief tastes like from within when it has become suffocating, private, or wounding.

vcgs.label: Aggregating VCG for Boundary Terms
vcgs.description: Aggregating VCG for BOUNDARY terms — pending researcher disposition at Phase 12. Members: 28 terms spanning press/affliction, torment/judgment, labour-pain, faintness/weakness, toil-suffering, mixed-register bitterness/complaint/distress vocabulary. No characteristic-bearing VCG design at Phase 7.
